summary: On September 11, 2001, I witnessed the first plane hit one of the towers at the World Trade Center while I was on Ellis Island. I saw flames coming out of the tower and later the second plane hit the other tower in a way that seemed unreal to me. Watching the towers burn and eventually collapse was devastating and surreal, and it completely shifted my focus from my usual work to the unfolding tragedy.

Being a native New Yorker, I initially thought any incident at the World Trade Center would be controlled quickly and not serious. However, as the events of September 11 unfolded, it became clear that New York City was at the center of a major tragedy. The city was filled with fear and uncertainty, especially as we heard rumors about other places being attacked and worried about the safety of landmarks and people.

I was on Ellis Island on the morning of September 11, 2001, working as the horticulturist when the attacks happened. We gathered at the flagpole area to stay safe and comfort each other as we watched the towers burn from a distance. Later, Ellis Island became a triage center where I helped by distributing water and comforting injured and shocked people brought over from Battery Park.

I recalled hearing about and seeing pictures of a plane hitting the Empire State Building years ago, which made me initially optimistic that the fires at the World Trade Center towers could be controlled. This memory gave me a false sense of security on September 11, 2001, before the full scale of the attacks became clear. It was a stark contrast to the reality I witnessed that day.

The September 11 attacks completely changed my day and my perspective. I was on Ellis Island when the planes hit the World Trade Center towers, and I experienced a delayed emotional reaction as I tried to process the horror. I helped set up a triage center and comforted people, focusing on others rather than myself, while the city and nation faced unprecedented fear and uncertainty.

After the attacks, injured and dust-covered people were brought over from Battery Park to Ellis Island, where I was helping at the triage center. Battery Park was a key location for evacuations and rescue efforts, and I remember giving out water and comforting those who arrived from there. It was a place connected to the immediate aftermath and emergency response following the attacks.

During the chaos of September 11, there were rumors that a building in Philadelphia had been hit, which added to the confusion and fear we felt on Ellis Island. These reports were mixed with other rumors about attacks in different places, making it hard to know what was true. It reflected the widespread uncertainty and misinformation circulating that day.

On September 11, we heard reports about a plane incident in Pennsylvania, which was part of the confusing and frightening information coming in. Although it was difficult to sort fact from rumor, the mention of Pennsylvania alongside places like Philadelphia and the Pentagon heightened our anxiety. It was one of several locations linked to the attacks in the early hours.

There were rumors on September 11 that something had happened at the White House, which contributed to the general fear and uncertainty we were experiencing on Ellis Island. Although these reports were not accurate, they reflected how many people believed multiple key sites were under attack. It was a tense time with many conflicting pieces of information.

We heard that the Pentagon had been hit during the attacks on September 11, which was one of the few confirmed reports amid many rumors. This news added to the gravity of the situation as it showed the attacks were not limited to New York City. The Pentagon's involvement made the event feel like a national crisis, affecting multiple important sites.

On September 11, while we were gathered near the flagpole on Ellis Island, a small motorboat approached quickly and made everyone very nervous, fearing a possible attack. The United States Coast Guard intercepted and stopped the boat, which helped calm our fears. Their presence was crucial in maintaining security during those tense and uncertain moments.
